title: Power & Liquid Systems
description: Power graph, consumers/producers, liquid handling modules, and block power interfaces

classes.name: PowerModule
fields: float status = 0.0f  // % power supply (0-1) or buffer fill %
methods: write(Writes write): void  // serializes links + status
read(Reads read): void     // deserializes links + status; NaN guard

classes.name: LiquidModule
fields: WindowedMean[] flow  // nullable; active only when flow tracking enabled
methods: updateFlow(): void         // polls WindowedMean, updates displayFlow
getFlowRate(Liquid): float // u/s; -1 if not ready
reset(Liquid, float): void // clears all, sets one liquid
add(Liquid, float): void   // adds amount; updates current + flow sums
handleFlow(Liquid, float): void  // tracks flow without modifying storage

classes.name: ConsumePower
fields: float usage     // max power consumed per tick
float capacity  // max storable power (buffered only)
methods: ignore(): boolean            // returns buffered (buffered consumers skip validity checks)

classes.name: PowerGraph
fields: PowerGraphUpdater entity  // nullable; drives update() each game tick
methods: update(): void              // main tick: getPowerNeeded/Produced → battery arbitrage → distributePower
getPowerNeeded(): float     // sums consPower.requestedPower * delta across consumers
getPowerProduced(): float   // sums getPowerProduction * delta across producers
useBatteries(float needed): float  // drains proportionally across all batteries
chargeBatteries(float excess): float // fills proportionally across all batteries
transferPower(float amount): void  // positive→charge, negative→drain; updates energyDelta
distributePower(float needed, float produced, boolean charged): void // sets power.status on all consumers
addGraph(PowerGraph): void  // BFS merge; smaller merges into larger
remove(Building): void      // BFS split into new sub-graphs
reflow(Building): void      // BFS re-add all connected buildings
classes.logic_notes: update() flow: if produced>needed → chargeBatteries(excess); if needed>produced → useBatteries(deficit); then distributePower(). Buffered consumers (batteries) get % of capacity per tick; unbuffered consumers get coverage ratio as status.

classes.name: Battery
classes.notes: Both outputsPower and consumesPower → classified as battery (buffered) in PowerGraph.add(). Uses ConsumePower with buffered=true.

inner_classes.name: BatteryBuild
methods: overwrote(Seq<Building>): void  // transfers power from replaced buffered buildings
status(): BlockStatus  // noInput if status≈0, active if status≈1, else noOutput

classes.name: LiquidBlock
static_methods: drawTiledFrames(int, float, float, float, Liquid, float): void

inner_classes.name: LiquidBuild
methods: draw(): void  // renders bottom, liquid fill, top

classes.name: Conduit
methods: blends(Tile, int, int, int, int, Block): boolean
getReplacement(BuildPlan, Seq<BuildPlan>): Block  // junction replacement logic

inner_classes.name: ConduitBuild
methods: updateTile(): void      // lerps smoothLiquid; calls moveLiquidForward(leaks, current) if has liquid
acceptLiquid(Building, Liquid): boolean  // checks liquid type match + source direction
onProximityUpdate(): void  // recalculates blend bits; sets capped/backCapped
drawAt(float, float, int, int, SliceMode): void
next(): Building  // returns ConduitBuild at front tile if present

key_relationships: PowerModule composes PowerGraph (1:1; each Building's power module holds its graph)
PowerModule holds IntSeq links (linked building positions)
PowerGraph composes PowerGraphUpdater entity (drives update() each tick via Entityc lifecycle)
PowerGraph uses ConsumePower via Building.block.consPower for requestedPower + capacity reads
ConsumePower reads PowerModule.status via Building.power.status in efficiency()
ConsumePower.apply(Block) sets Block.consPower reference and Block.hasPower=true
Battery (Block) has ConsumePower with buffered=true → classified as battery in PowerGraph.add()
Building holds PowerModule as .power field (hasPower blocks)
Building holds LiquidModule as .liquids field (hasLiquids blocks)
ConduitBuild.updateTile() calls moveLiquidForward() which operates on LiquidModule
LiquidBlock.LiquidBuild reads LiquidModule.currentAmount() and current() for rendering

PowerGraph.add(Building) categorizes: outputsPower && consumesPower && !buffered → producer+consumer; outputsPower && consumesPower && buffered → battery; outputsPower only → producer; consumesPower only → consumer
key_logic_flows: PowerGraph.update(): compute needed+produced → if surplus chargeBatteries → if deficit useBatteries → distributePower sets consumer power.status (coverage ratio or buffer %)
PowerGraph.reflow(tile): BFS traversal of getPowerConnections() to rebuild graph membership
PowerGraph.remove(tile): BFS splits remaining connected components into new PowerGraph instances
Battery.BatteryBuild.overwrote(): inherits stored power from replaced buffered buildings
Conduit.ConduitBuild.updateTile(): smoothLiquid lerp + moveLiquidForward() with leak support
ConsumePower.requestedPower(): buffered → requests (1-status)*capacity per tick; unbuffered → usage if shouldConsume else 0
important_notes: PowerGraphUpdater is a generated entity (from PowerGraphUpdaterComp annotation component) — one per PowerGraph. PowerModule.graph is reassigned when graphs merge (addGraph BFS). Battery sets update=false because power balance is driven by PowerGraphUpdater, not by the Battery building's own update. LiquidModule uses static cache arrays (cacheFlow, cacheSums, displayFlow) shared across all modules for GC efficiency — only the active module borrows them per poll tick.
